title: Chiogenes hispidula
taxonomy: Chiogenes hispidula is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is a small to medium-sized shrub native to New Zealand. Within the Ericaceae family, Chiogenes hispidula is placed in the subfamily Epacridoideae. This subfamily is part of the larger order Ericales, which comprises a diverse group of flowering plants. The Ericaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Chiogenes hispidula belonging to the Epacridoideae subfamily.

It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. intro: Chiogenes hispidula, also known as sea spurrey, is a coastal succulent plant native to the Mediterranean region and western Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, providing relief from various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
